perturbation.original_text: Recipient will cooperate with FormFactor in every reasonable way to help FormFactor regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not obligated to cooperate with FormFactor to regain possession of the Confidential Information or prevent further unauthorized use.
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the Recipient's obligation to cooperate with FormFactor, potentially hindering FormFactor's ability to protect its confidential information. This directly contradicts the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing present in contracts and the duty to mitigate damages.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1708 states, "Every person is bound, without contract, to abstain from injuring the person or property of another, or infringing upon any of his rights." By removing the obligation to cooperate in preventing unauthorized use of confidential information, the modified text allows the recipient to potentially infringe upon FormFactor's property rights (the confidential information) without consequence, contradicting this code. While the code doesn't explicitly mandate contractual cooperation, removing such a clause could be interpreted as a failure to 'abstain from injuring' FormFactor's rights when unauthorized use occurs.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1708 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 3. OBLIGATIONS IMPOSED BY LAW [1708 - 1725] ( Part 3 enacted 1872. ) 1708. Every person is bound, without contract, to abstain from injuring the person or property of another, or infringing upon any of his or her rights. (Amended by Stats. 2002, Ch. 664, Sec. 38.5. Effective January 1, 2003.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the recipient's obligation to cooperate with FormFactor to protect its confidential information. This directly contradicts California Civil Code § 1708, which states that "Every person is bound, without contract, to abstain from injuring the person or property of another, or infringing upon any of his or her rights." By removing the obligation to cooperate, the recipient could potentially infringe upon FormFactor's property rights (confidential information) without consequence, which is a violation of the code.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Within thirty (365) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This modification extends the return period of confidential information to 365 business days, or approximately one year, which is unreasonable and could violate California's Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Uniform Trade Secrets Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 3426–3426.11)
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code Section 3426.2 allows for injunctive relief to prevent misappropriation of trade secrets. Delaying the return of confidential information for a year would likely constitute misappropriation, as it deprives FormFactor of its ability to control and protect its trade secrets during that time.  The extended timeframe makes it more difficult for FormFactor to prevent unauthorized use or disclosure and, thus, conflicts with the intent of the law to provide timely remedies for trade secret violations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 3426.2 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT [3426 - 3426.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1. ) 3426.2. (a) Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. (b) If the court determines that it would be unreasonable to prohibit future use, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time the use could have been prohibited. (c) In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. (Added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the return period of confidential information to 365 business days, which contradicts California Civil Code Section 3426.2. The law states that 'Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined.' Allowing a year before returning confidential information increases the risk of misappropriation, which undermines the purpose of the law to protect trade secrets. The change directly conflicts with the statute's intent to provide injunctive relief against misappropriation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The parties hereby agree to submit themselves to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts within Santa Clara County, California.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The parties hereby agree to submit themselves to the jurisdiction of the courts within Seychelles.
perturbation.explanation: While parties can generally agree to a choice of law and forum, choosing a forum with no connection to the parties or the agreement can be challenged, especially if it prejudices one party's ability to litigate.  Selecting Seychelles raises enforceability issues and could violate California public policy if it unduly burdens FormFactor.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Code of Civil Procedure Section 410.30 allows California courts to stay or dismiss actions when, in the interest of substantial justice, the action should be heard in a forum outside California. While this statute doesn't directly prohibit parties from agreeing to a foreign forum, a California court could refuse to enforce a forum selection clause that designates a forum like Seychelles if it determines that litigating there would be highly inconvenient or unjust for the California-based party (FormFactor), considering their connection to California and the lack of connection to Seychelles.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CCP 410.30 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Code of Civil Procedure - CCP PART 2. OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. JURISDICTION AND SERVICE OF PROCESS [410.10 - 418.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1969, Ch. 1610. ) CHAPTER 1. Jurisdiction and Forum [410.10 - 410.70] ( Heading of Chapter 1 amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 615, Sec. 3. ) ARTICLE 2. Forum [410.30 - 410.42] ( Heading of Article 2 amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 615, Sec. 4. ) 410.30. (a) When a court upon motion of a party or its own motion finds that in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in a forum outside this state, the court shall stay or dismiss the action in whole or in part on any conditions that may be just. (b) The provisions of Section 418.10 do not apply to a motion to stay or dismiss the action by a defendant who has made a general appearance. (Amended by Stats. 1972, Ch. 601. Note: This version was suspended from Sept. 22, 1986, until Jan. 1, 1992, during operation of the temporary amendment by Stats. 1986, Ch. 968.)
perturbation.justification: The original contract designates California law and Santa Clara County courts for dispute resolution. The changed contract selects Delaware law and Seychelles courts. The Law Explanation indicates this forum selection could be problematic if it is highly inconvenient or unjust for the California-based party. Scraped Law Snippet 1, CCP 410.30(a), allows a California court to stay or dismiss an action if it should be heard in a forum outside the state in the interest of substantial justice. By changing the forum to Seychelles, which likely has no connection to the parties or the transaction, the contract creates a situation where a California court could refuse to enforce the forum selection clause, thus contradicting the original intent and potentially violating California public policy as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This undermines the enforceability within California, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for one (1) year from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, one (1) year following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period to one year may be insufficient to protect FormFactor's trade secrets, potentially violating California's Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Uniform Trade Secrets Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 3426–3426.11)
perturbation.law_explanation: California's Uniform Trade Secrets Act (CUTSA) defines a trade secret broadly and provides remedies for misappropriation. While CUTSA doesn't explicitly mandate a minimum confidentiality period, shortening it to one year may be deemed insufficient to protect information that requires a longer period to maintain its competitive edge. If the one-year period is deemed unreasonable given the nature of the trade secrets involved, FormFactor may not have adequate legal recourse to prevent misappropriation after the year has passed. A court could find that this unreasonably short period does not provide adequate protection for legitimate trade secrets, potentially conflicting with the overall intent of CUTSA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 3426 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT [3426 - 3426.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1. ) 3426. This title may be cited as the Uniform Trade Secrets Act. (Added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period from five years to one year. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both state that this reduction may be insufficient to protect FormFactor's trade secrets under California's Uniform Trade Secrets Act (CUTSA). Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that the title can be cited as the Uniform Trade Secrets Act, the Law Explanation clarifies that the CUTSA doesn't mandate a minimum confidentiality period, shortening it to one year could still be deemed insufficient to protect information. Given that the modified clause could render the contract non-compliant by not providing adequate protection for trade secrets, the contradiction exists.
